$comment: One engine-level finding-disposition record (docs/finding-dispositions.md): the author's decision to set a ledger finding aside — declined (a deliberate pass) or deferred (re-openable; a recorded trigger resurfaces it). A disposition is an OVERLAY on the finding lifecycle, NOT a lifecycle state: execution.finding_dispositions maps F-id -> this record beside (never inside) execution.finding_states, and finding_states stays exactly {locked, delivered, revised} (finding-trace E3, fold monotonicity untouched). finding_states[id] == 'revised' SUPERSEDES any disposition for id (read-time precedence; the record is kept, never deleted). Closed-key with deliberately NO severity field — a disposition structurally cannot carry severity relief (the anti-laundering property; disposition-check DP2.3/DP2.4). The cross-field rule the stdlib subset checker cannot express — `trigger` required iff disposition == 'deferred' — lives in disposition-check DP0 (artifact-side) and run_gate.py --check-state (log-side), the same split as apodictic.gate_event.v1's cross-field rules. Validated by scripts/disposition_check.py (validate.sh disposition-check); on governed projects the records ride gate_events[].disposition_deltas and fold into the sidecar.
title: APODICTIC finding disposition (apodictic.finding_disposition.v1)

trigger.$comment: required iff disposition == 'deferred' (DP0); an ISO date (YYYY-MM / YYYY-MM-DD) fires mechanically in the coach's trigger review, any other text is coach-judged

artifact.$comment: optional provenance: which file carried the marker
